message:
perf(memdb): borrow indexed-equality rowids and add SimpleCountStar+dense-rowid fast paths

Three coordinated perf changes inside the memdb-backed query fast paths.
All keep external behaviour identical and are validated with focused
unit tests in their respective crates.

1) Replace prepared_indexed_equality_rowids() with a callback-style
   with_prepared_indexed_equality_rowids(..., f: impl FnOnce(&[i64]) -> R)
   so callers consume the cached rowid list as a borrowed slice instead
   of cloning it into a Box<[i64]>. The two callers in this crate that
   previously cloned (the indexed-equality projection path and the
   single-row collection path) now do their work inside the closure
   while holding the cache borrow, then drop it.

   The cache-miss branch is restructured so the new entry is inserted
   into the RefCell, the &mut borrow released, then a fresh & borrow
   reads the entry back to feed the closure — avoids the previous
   pattern of cloning out of the cache so the &mut RefMut could be
   dropped.

   Net effect on a hot indexed-equality lookup: removes one Box<[i64]>
   clone per call site per row probe.

   prepared_indexed_equality_rowid_count() is now a thin call to
   with_prepared_indexed_equality_rowids(... |rowids| rowids.len()),
   which both deduplicates the cache-key build and gets the same
   no-clone behaviour for free.

2) New PreparedQueryFastPath::SimpleCountStar arm in
   try_prepared_query_against_memdb(). When
   memdb_storage_count_shortcuts_safe is set, a `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t`
   plan resolves to a single Row containing table.row_count() as i64,
   bypassing the full row materialization. i64::try_from is checked so
   pathological table sizes surface as an Internal error rather than
   silently truncating.

3) Track cached_write_txn_memdb_row_mirror_exact across both the
   schema-preserving and full reload paths in
   refresh_memdb_during_cached_write_txn(). The flag is set to true
   only when reload_memdb_rows_from_txn_preserving_schema succeeds, or
   to the value of hydrate_rows when reload_memdb_from_txn_with_mode
   succeeds. Errors leave the flag at its previous value, which is
   what later count/lookup shortcuts gate on. Without this, a
   successful schema-preserving refresh wasn't being recognized as
   "rows in memdb match storage" and downstream shortcut paths fell
   through unnecessarily.

4) MemTable::find_by_rowid() now tries a dense O(1) lookup before the
   binary search via a new dense_rowid_offset() helper. The helper:
   - Reads the table's first rowid.
   - Computes (target - first) with checked_sub (so wrap is impossible
     for unusual rowid distributions).
   - usize::try_from()s the offset (filters negative-offset and
     overflow cases).
   - Indexes self.rows directly, then verifies row.rowid == target so
     the dense path can never claim a wrong row in a sparse table.
   On dense tables (the common case for the autoincrement-style rowids
   we generate) this turns find_by_rowid into a constant-time slice
   index. Sparse tables fall through to the existing binary search,
   which the new test covers explicitly: insert 10/11/12, delete 11,
   confirm the binary-search fallback still resolves rowid 12 after
   the dense offset path returns None for the deleted middle row.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
